instruction: Implement Google Test patterns for C++ unit testing, including test fixtures, parameterized tests, typed tests, and death tests for assertion checking.
output:
<think>
Google Test (gtest) is the standard C++ testing framework. Key features:
1. TEST() macro for simple tests
2. TEST_F() for tests using fixtures (shared setup/teardown)
3. TEST_P() for parameterized tests (same test, different inputs)
4. TYPED_TEST() for testing template classes with different types
5. EXPECT_DEATH() for testing that code crashes/aborts as expected
6. Custom matchers for expressive assertions

I'll test a real data structure (a bounded buffer) and a parser, showing all these patterns.

Fixture pattern: SetUp()/TearDown() run before/after each test. SetUpTestSuite()/TearDownTestSuite() run once per test suite.

Parameterized tests are great for edge cases — define test logic once, run with many inputs. The values come from ::testing::Values, ValuesIn, Combine, or Range.
</think>

```cpp
#include <gtest/gtest.h>
#include <gmock/gmock.h>
#include <memory>
#include <optional>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

// --- Code under test ---

template <typename T>
class BoundedBuffer {
    std::vector<T> buffer_;
    size_t capacity_;
    size_t head_ = 0, tail_ = 0, size_ = 0;

public:
    explicit BoundedBuffer(size_t capacity)
        : buffer_(capacity), capacity_(capacity) {
        if (capacity == 0) throw std::invalid_argument("capacity must be > 0");
    }

    void push(const T& item) {
        if (size_ >= capacity_) throw std::overflow_error("buffer full");
        buffer_[tail_] = item;
        tail_ = (tail_ + 1) % capacity_;
        ++size_;
    }

    T pop() {
        if (size_ == 0) throw std::underflow_error("buffer empty");
        T item = buffer_[head_];
        head_ = (head_ + 1) % capacity_;
        --size_;
        return item;
    }

    size_t size() const { return size_; }
    bool empty() const { return size_ == 0; }
    bool full() const { return size_ >= capacity_; }
    size_t capacity() const { return capacity_; }
};

struct ParseResult {
    std::string key;
    std::string value;
    bool valid;
};

ParseResult parseKeyValue(const std::string& line) {
    auto pos = line.find('=');
    if (pos == std::string::npos) return {"", "", false};
    std::string key = line.substr(0, pos);
    std::string value = line.substr(pos + 1);
    if (key.empty()) return {"", "", false};
    return {key, value, true};
}

// --- Test Fixture ---

class BoundedBufferTest : public ::testing::Test {
protected:
    std::unique_ptr<BoundedBuffer<int>> buffer;

    void SetUp() override {
        buffer = std::make_unique<BoundedBuffer<int>>(5);
    }

    void fillBuffer() {
        for (int i = 0; i < 5; ++i) {
            buffer->push(i * 10);
        }
    }
};

TEST_F(BoundedBufferTest, StartsEmpty) {
    EXPECT_TRUE(buffer->empty());
    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->size(), 0u);
    EXPECT_FALSE(buffer->full());
}

TEST_F(BoundedBufferTest, PushIncreasesSize) {
    buffer->push(42);
    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->size(), 1u);
    EXPECT_FALSE(buffer->empty());
}

TEST_F(BoundedBufferTest, FIFOOrder) {
    buffer->push(1);
    buffer->push(2);
    buffer->push(3);
    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->pop(), 1);
    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->pop(), 2);
    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->pop(), 3);
}

TEST_F(BoundedBufferTest, FullBufferThrows) {
    fillBuffer();
    EXPECT_TRUE(buffer->full());
    EXPECT_THROW(buffer->push(999), std::overflow_error);
}

TEST_F(BoundedBufferTest, EmptyPopThrows) {
    EXPECT_THROW(buffer->pop(), std::underflow_error);
}

TEST_F(BoundedBufferTest, WrapAround) {
    // Fill and drain partially, then fill again to test circular behavior
    for (int i = 0; i < 5; ++i) buffer->push(i);
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) buffer->pop();
    for (int i = 5; i < 8; ++i) buffer->push(i);

    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->size(), 5u);
    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->pop(), 3);
    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->pop(), 4);
    EXPECT_EQ(buffer->pop(), 5);
}

// --- Parameterized Tests ---

struct KeyValueCase {
    std::string input;
    bool expected_valid;
    std::string expected_key;
    std::string expected_value;
};

class ParseKeyValueTest : public ::testing::TestWithParam<KeyValueCase> {};

TEST_P(ParseKeyValueTest, ParsesCorrectly) {
    auto [input, expected_valid, expected_key, expected_value] = GetParam();
    auto result = parseKeyValue(input);

    EXPECT_EQ(result.valid, expected_valid) << "Input: " << input;
    if (expected_valid) {
        EXPECT_EQ(result.key, expected_key);
        EXPECT_EQ(result.value, expected_value);
    }
}

INSTANTIATE_TEST_SUITE_P(
    ValidCases, ParseKeyValueTest,
    ::testing::Values(
        KeyValueCase{"name=Alice", true, "name", "Alice"},
        KeyValueCase{"key=value", true, "key", "value"},
        KeyValueCase{"x=", true, "x", ""},
        KeyValueCase{"path=/usr/bin", true, "path", "/usr/bin"},
        KeyValueCase{"a=b=c", true, "a", "b=c"}  // Only first = splits
    )
);

INSTANTIATE_TEST_SUITE_P(
    InvalidCases, ParseKeyValueTest,
    ::testing::Values(
        KeyValueCase{"no_equals", false, "", ""},
        KeyValueCase{"=no_key", false, "", ""},
        KeyValueCase{"", false, "", ""}
    )
);

// --- Typed Tests (template testing) ---

template <typename T>
class BoundedBufferTypedTest : public ::testing::Test {
protected:
    BoundedBuffer<T> buffer{10};
};

using BufferTypes = ::testing::Types<int, double, std::string>;
TYPED_TEST_SUITE(BoundedBufferTypedTest, BufferTypes);

TYPED_TEST(BoundedBufferTypedTest, PushPopRoundTrip) {
    TypeParam value{};
    if constexpr (std::is_same_v<TypeParam, std::string>) {
        value = "hello";
    } else {
        value = static_cast<TypeParam>(42);
    }

    this->buffer.push(value);
    auto result = this->buffer.pop();
    EXPECT_EQ(result, value);
}

TYPED_TEST(BoundedBufferTypedTest, MultipleRoundTrips) {
    for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i) {
        TypeParam value{};
        if constexpr (std::is_same_v<TypeParam, std::string>) {
            value = std::to_string(i);
        } else {
            value = static_cast<TypeParam>(i);
        }
        this->buffer.push(value);
    }
    EXPECT_TRUE(this->buffer.full());
}

// --- Death Tests ---

TEST(BoundedBufferDeathTest, ZeroCapacityAborts) {
    EXPECT_THROW(BoundedBuffer<int>(0), std::invalid_argument);
}

// --- Custom Matchers (GMock) ---

using ::testing::AllOf;
using ::testing::Field;
using ::testing::HasSubstr;

MATCHER_P(IsValidWith, key, "is valid with key " + std::string(key)) {
    return arg.valid && arg.key == key;
}

TEST(ParseKeyValueMatcher, UsesCustomMatcher) {
    auto result = parseKeyValue("host=localhost");
    EXPECT_THAT(result, IsValidWith("host"));
    EXPECT_THAT(result, AllOf(
        Field(&ParseResult::valid, true),
        Field(&ParseResult::key, "host"),
        Field(&ParseResult::value, "localhost")
    ));
}
```

instruction: Implement C++ sanitizer integration for testing, including AddressSanitizer for memory errors, ThreadSanitizer for data races, and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with CMake integration.
output:
<think>
Sanitizers are compiler instrumentation tools that detect bugs at runtime. They're the most valuable testing tool for C++ after unit tests because they find bugs that tests pass but production crashes on.

Three main sanitizers:
1. AddressSanitizer (ASan): heap buffer overflow, use-after-free, stack overflow, memory leaks
2. ThreadSanitizer (TSan): data races, deadlocks
3. UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (UBSan): signed integer overflow, null pointer deref, alignment issues

They can't all run together — ASan and TSan are incompatible. UBSan can combine with either.

CMake integration: add compiler/linker flags conditionally based on a build option. Create separate build configurations.

I'll show:
1. CMake setup with sanitizer options
2. Example code with intentional bugs that sanitizers catch
3. How to interpret sanitizer output
4. CI integration patterns
</think>

```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt — Sanitizer integration

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(sanitizer_demo CXX)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)

# Sanitizer options
option(ENABLE_ASAN "Enable AddressSanitizer" OFF)
option(ENABLE_TSAN "Enable ThreadSanitizer" OFF)
option(ENABLE_UBSAN "Enable UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer" OFF)
option(ENABLE_MSAN "Enable MemorySanitizer" OFF)

# Sanitizer flags
if(ENABLE_ASAN)
    message(STATUS "AddressSanitizer enabled")
    add_compile_options(-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g)
    add_link_options(-fsanitize=address)
endif()

if(ENABLE_TSAN)
    if(ENABLE_ASAN)
        message(FATAL_ERROR "ASan and TSan cannot be used together")
    endif()
    message(STATUS "ThreadSanitizer enabled")
    add_compile_options(-fsanitize=thread -g)
    add_link_options(-fsanitize=thread)
endif()

if(ENABLE_UBSAN)
    message(STATUS "UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer enabled")
    add_compile_options(
        -fsanitize=undefined
        -fsanitize=integer            # Signed/unsigned overflow
        -fsanitize=nullability        # Null pointer issues
        -fno-sanitize-recover=all     # Abort on first UB (don't just warn)
        -g
    )
    add_link_options(-fsanitize=undefined -fsanitize=integer)
endif()

# Source files
add_executable(tests
    test_main.cpp
)

target_link_libraries(tests PRIVATE gtest gtest_main pthread)

enable_testing()
add_test(NAME unit_tests COMMAND tests)
```

```cpp
// test_main.cpp — Tests that sanitizers catch

#include <gtest/gtest.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <cstring>
#include <memory>
#include <numeric>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

// ===== AddressSanitizer catches these =====

class SafeBuffer {
    std::unique_ptr<int[]> data_;
    size_t size_;

public:
    SafeBuffer(size_t size) : data_(std::make_unique<int[]>(size)), size_(size) {
        std::memset(data_.get(), 0, size * sizeof(int));
    }

    int& at(size_t index) {
        if (index >= size_) throw std::out_of_range("index out of bounds");
        return data_[index];
    }

    // Unsafe access — ASan catches buffer overflow
    int& operator[](size_t index) {
        return data_[index];  // No bounds check!
    }

    size_t size() const { return size_; }
};

TEST(AddressSanitizer, BoundsCheckedAccess) {
    SafeBuffer buf(10);
    buf.at(0) = 42;
    EXPECT_EQ(buf.at(0), 42);
    EXPECT_THROW(buf.at(10), std::out_of_range);
}

// This test would be caught by ASan if uncommented:
// TEST(AddressSanitizer, HeapBufferOverflow) {
//     SafeBuffer buf(10);
//     buf[10] = 999;  // ASan: heap-buffer-overflow
// }

// Correct version that ASan validates is clean:
TEST(AddressSanitizer, NoLeaks) {
    auto ptr = std::make_unique<std::vector<int>>(1000, 42);
    EXPECT_EQ(ptr->size(), 1000u);
    // unique_ptr auto-cleans — no leak
}

// ===== ThreadSanitizer catches these =====

class ThreadSafeCounter {
    std::atomic<int> count_{0};

public:
    void increment() { count_.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed); }
    int get() const { return count_.load(std::memory_order_relaxed); }
};

TEST(ThreadSanitizer, AtomicCounterIsRaceFree) {
    ThreadSafeCounter counter;
    constexpr int num_threads = 8;
    constexpr int increments = 10000;

    std::vector<std::thread> threads;
    for (int i = 0; i < num_threads; ++i) {
        threads.emplace_back([&]() {
            for (int j = 0; j < increments; ++j) {
                counter.increment();
            }
        });
    }
    for (auto& t : threads) t.join();

    EXPECT_EQ(counter.get(), num_threads * increments);
}

// Unsafe version that TSan would catch:
// class UnsafeCounter {
//     int count_ = 0;  // No atomic!
// public:
//     void increment() { ++count_; }  // TSan: data race
//     int get() const { return count_; }
// };

// ===== UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer catches these =====

int safe_add(int a, int b) {
    // Check for overflow before it happens
    if (b > 0 && a > INT_MAX - b) throw std::overflow_error("integer overflow");
    if (b < 0 && a < INT_MIN - b) throw std::overflow_error("integer underflow");
    return a + b;
}

TEST(UBSan, SafeIntegerArithmetic) {
    EXPECT_EQ(safe_add(1, 2), 3);
    EXPECT_EQ(safe_add(-1, 1), 0);
    EXPECT_THROW(safe_add(INT_MAX, 1), std::overflow_error);
    EXPECT_THROW(safe_add(INT_MIN, -1), std::overflow_error);
}

// UBSan would catch this if uncommented:
// TEST(UBSan, SignedOverflow) {
//     int x = INT_MAX;
//     x += 1;  // UBSan: signed integer overflow
// }

TEST(UBSan, NullPointerDereference) {
    int* ptr = nullptr;
    EXPECT_EQ(ptr, nullptr);  // Fine
    // *ptr = 42;  // UBSan: null pointer dereference
}

// Safe division
int safe_divide(int a, int b) {
    if (b == 0) throw std::domain_error("division by zero");
    if (a == INT_MIN && b == -1) throw std::overflow_error("overflow");
    return a / b;
}

TEST(UBSan, SafeDivision) {
    EXPECT_EQ(safe_divide(10, 2), 5);
    EXPECT_THROW(safe_divide(1, 0), std::domain_error);
    EXPECT_THROW(safe_divide(INT_MIN, -1), std::overflow_error);
}

/*
Build and run with sanitizers:

  # AddressSanitizer
  cmake -B build-asan -DENABLE_ASAN=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug .
  cmake --build build-asan
  cd build-asan && ctest --output-on-failure

  # ThreadSanitizer
  cmake -B build-tsan -DENABLE_TSAN=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug .
  cmake --build build-tsan
  cd build-tsan && ctest --output-on-failure

  # UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer
  cmake -B build-ubsan -DENABLE_UBSAN=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug .
  cmake --build build-ubsan
  cd build-ubsan && ctest --output-on-failure

  # CI: run all three in parallel
  parallel cmake -B build-{} -DENABLE_{}=ON . ::: asan tsan ubsan
*/
```

instruction: Implement Google Benchmark framework patterns for C++ micro-benchmarking with custom counters, argument ranges, and complexity analysis.
output:
<think>
Google Benchmark is the standard C++ micro-benchmarking framework. It's more sophisticated than simple timing loops because:
1. Auto-calibrates iteration count
2. Reports statistical measures (mean, median, stddev)
3. Supports custom counters (throughput, items/sec)
4. Can analyze algorithmic complexity (O(n), O(n log n))
5. Supports fixtures, parameterized benchmarks, and threading

Key patterns:
- BENCHMARK(fn): register a benchmark
- state.range(0): get the argument for parameterized benchmarks
- state.SetBytesProcessed(): enable throughput reporting
- state.SetComplexityN(): set N for complexity analysis
- state.SetItemsProcessed(): report items/sec
- Benchmark::Arg/Range/DenseRange/ArgsProduct: control inputs

I'll benchmark real operations: sorting, hashing, string operations, and container access patterns.
</think>

```cpp
#include <benchmark/benchmark.h>
#include <algorithm>
#include <numeric>
#include <random>
#include <string>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <vector>
#include <map>

// --- Sorting: Complexity Analysis ---

static void BM_StdSort(benchmark::State& state) {
    std::mt19937 rng(42);
    const auto n = state.range(0);
    std::vector<int> data(n);

    for (auto _ : state) {
        state.PauseTiming();
        std::iota(data.begin(), data.end(), 0);
        std::shuffle(data.begin(), data.end(), rng);
        state.ResumeTiming();

        std::sort(data.begin(), data.end());
        benchmark::DoNotOptimize(data.data());
    }

    state.SetComplexityN(n);
    state.SetItemsProcessed(state.iterations() * n);
}
BENCHMARK(BM_StdSort)
    ->RangeMultiplier(4)
    ->Range(64, 1 << 20)
    ->Complexity(benchmark::oNLogN);  // Expect O(n log n)

// --- Container Lookup Comparison ---

static void BM_MapLookup(benchmark::State& state) {
    const auto n = state.range(0);
    std::map<int, int> m;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) m[i] = i;

    std::mt19937 rng(42);
    for (auto _ : state) {
        int key = rng() % n;
        auto it = m.find(key);
        benchmark::DoNotOptimize(it);
    }

    state.SetComplexityN(n);
}
BENCHMARK(BM_MapLookup)
    ->RangeMultiplier(4)
    ->Range(64, 1 << 20)
    ->Complexity(benchmark::oLogN);

static void BM_UnorderedMapLookup(benchmark::State& state) {
    const auto n = state.range(0);
    std::unordered_map<int, int> m;
    m.reserve(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) m[i] = i;

    std::mt19937 rng(42);
    for (auto _ : state) {
        int key = rng() % n;
        auto it = m.find(key);
        benchmark::DoNotOptimize(it);
    }

    state.SetComplexityN(n);
}
BENCHMARK(BM_UnorderedMapLookup)
    ->RangeMultiplier(4)
    ->Range(64, 1 << 20)
    ->Complexity(benchmark::o1);

// --- String Operations with Throughput ---

static void BM_StringConcat(benchmark::State& state) {
    const auto n = state.range(0);
    for (auto _ : state) {
        std::string result;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            result += "hello";
        }
        benchmark::DoNotOptimize(result);
    }

    state.SetBytesProcessed(state.iterations() * n * 5);
    state.counters["strings"] = benchmark::Counter(
        state.iterations() * n,
        benchmark::Counter::kIsRate  // Display as per-second
    );
}
BENCHMARK(BM_StringConcat)->Range(1, 1 << 16);

static void BM_StringReserveConcat(benchmark::State& state) {
    const auto n = state.range(0);
    for (auto _ : state) {
        std::string result;
        result.reserve(n * 5);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            result += "hello";
        }
        benchmark::DoNotOptimize(result);
    }

    state.SetBytesProcessed(state.iterations() * n * 5);
}
BENCHMARK(BM_StringReserveConcat)->Range(1, 1 << 16);

// --- Multi-argument Benchmarks ---

static void BM_MatrixMultiply(benchmark::State& state) {
    const auto rows = state.range(0);
    const auto cols = state.range(1);

    std::vector<double> a(rows * cols, 1.0);
    std::vector<double> b(cols * rows, 2.0);
    std::vector<double> c(rows * rows, 0.0);

    for (auto _ : state) {
        for (int i = 0; i < rows; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < rows; ++j) {
                double sum = 0;
                for (int k = 0; k < cols; ++k) {
                    sum += a[i * cols + k] * b[k * rows + j];
                }
                c[i * rows + j] = sum;
            }
        }
        benchmark::DoNotOptimize(c.data());
        benchmark::ClobberMemory();
    }

    state.counters["FLOPS"] = benchmark::Counter(
        state.iterations() * 2.0 * rows * rows * cols,
        benchmark::Counter::kIsRate
    );
}
BENCHMARK(BM_MatrixMultiply)
    ->ArgsProduct({{32, 64, 128, 256}, {32, 64, 128, 256}})
    ->Unit(benchmark::kMillisecond);

// --- Fixture Benchmark ---

class DatabaseFixture : public benchmark::Fixture {
protected:
    std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> db_;

    void SetUp(const benchmark::State& state) override {
        const auto n = state.range(0);
        db_.reserve(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            db_["key_" + std::to_string(i)] = "value_" + std::to_string(i);
        }
    }

    void TearDown(const benchmark::State&) override {
        db_.clear();
    }
};

BENCHMARK_DEFINE_F(DatabaseFixture, ReadExisting)(benchmark::State& state) {
    std::mt19937 rng(42);
    const auto n = state.range(0);

    for (auto _ : state) {
        auto key = "key_" + std::to_string(rng() % n);
        auto it = db_.find(key);
        benchmark::DoNotOptimize(it->second);
    }

    state.SetItemsProcessed(state.iterations());
}
BENCHMARK_REGISTER_F(DatabaseFixture, ReadExisting)
    ->RangeMultiplier(10)
    ->Range(100, 1'000'000);

BENCHMARK_MAIN();

/*
Compile and run:
  g++ -O2 -o bench bench.cpp -lbenchmark -lpthread
  ./bench --benchmark_format=console
  ./bench --benchmark_format=json --benchmark_out=results.json

  # Compare two runs:
  pip install google-benchmark
  compare.py --no-color filters old.json new.json
*/
```

instruction: Implement C++ fuzz testing with libFuzzer, including custom mutators, corpus management, and integration with sanitizers for maximum bug-finding effectiveness.
output:
<think>
Fuzz testing is automated randomized testing. libFuzzer is LLVM's coverage-guided fuzzer — it generates random inputs, runs them through your code, and uses code coverage feedback to generate inputs that explore new code paths. Combined with sanitizers (ASan, UBSan), it finds bugs that no amount of manual testing would catch.

Key concepts:
1. Fuzz target: a function `LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput(const uint8_t*, size_t)` that takes random bytes
2. Coverage-guided: libFuzzer instruments the binary to track which code paths were hit
3. Corpus: a directory of inputs that achieved new coverage (persisted between runs)
4. Custom mutators: domain-specific mutation strategies for structured inputs
5. Sanitizers: compile with ASan+UBSan to detect bugs at runtime

I'll show:
- Basic fuzz target for a parser
- Custom mutator for structured input (JSON-like)
- Corpus management and dictionary usage
- CMake integration
- How to interpret findings
</think>

```cpp
// fuzz_parser.cpp — Basic fuzz target for a URL parser

#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>
#include <optional>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

// --- Code under test (simplified URL parser) ---

struct Url {
    std::string scheme;
    std::string host;
    uint16_t port;
    std::string path;
};

std::optional<Url> parse_url(std::string_view input) {
    Url url;

    // Scheme
    auto scheme_end = input.find("://");
    if (scheme_end == std::string_view::npos) return std::nullopt;
    url.scheme = std::string(input.substr(0, scheme_end));
    input.remove_prefix(scheme_end + 3);

    // Host and optional port
    auto path_start = input.find('/');
    auto host_part = (path_start != std::string_view::npos)
        ? input.substr(0, path_start)
        : input;

    auto colon = host_part.find(':');
    if (colon != std::string_view::npos) {
        url.host = std::string(host_part.substr(0, colon));
        auto port_str = host_part.substr(colon + 1);
        try {
            auto port = std::stoul(std::string(port_str));
            if (port > 65535) return std::nullopt;
            url.port = static_cast<uint16_t>(port);
        } catch (...) {
            return std::nullopt;
        }
    } else {
        url.host = std::string(host_part);
        url.port = 0;
    }

    // Path
    if (path_start != std::string_view::npos) {
        url.path = std::string(input.substr(path_start));
    } else {
        url.path = "/";
    }

    return url;
}

// --- Fuzz target ---

extern "C" int LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput(const uint8_t* data, size_t size) {
    // Don't waste time on huge inputs
    if (size > 4096) return 0;

    std::string_view input(reinterpret_cast<const char*>(data), size);
    auto result = parse_url(input);

    // If parsing succeeded, verify basic invariants
    if (result) {
        // Scheme should not be empty
        assert(!result->scheme.empty());
        // Host should not be empty
        assert(!result->host.empty());
        // Port should be in valid range
        assert(result->port <= 65535);
        // Path should start with / if non-empty
        if (!result->path.empty()) {
            assert(result->path[0] == '/');
        }
    }

    return 0;
}

// fuzz_json.cpp — Fuzz target with custom mutator for structured input

// Custom mutator that understands JSON-like structure
extern "C" size_t LLVMFuzzerCustomMutator(
    uint8_t* data, size_t size, size_t max_size, unsigned int seed
) {
    // Start with standard mutations
    size_t new_size = LLVMFuzzerMutate(data, size, max_size);

    // Occasionally inject JSON structural elements
    std::mt19937 rng(seed);
    if (rng() % 4 == 0 && new_size + 10 < max_size) {
        const char* tokens[] = {
            "{", "}", "[", "]", ":", ",",
            "\"key\"", "\"value\"",
            "null", "true", "false",
            "42", "-1", "3.14",
        };
        const char* token = tokens[rng() % (sizeof(tokens) / sizeof(tokens[0]))];
        size_t token_len = strlen(token);

        // Insert at random position
        size_t pos = rng() % (new_size + 1);
        if (new_size + token_len <= max_size) {
            memmove(data + pos + token_len, data + pos, new_size - pos);
            memcpy(data + pos, token, token_len);
            new_size += token_len;
        }
    }

    return new_size;
}
```

```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt for fuzz targets

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(fuzz_tests CXX)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)

# Fuzzing requires Clang
if(NOT CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "Clang")
    message(WARNING "Fuzzing requires Clang compiler")
endif()

# Fuzz build type
set(FUZZ_FLAGS
    -fsanitize=fuzzer,address,undefined
    -fno-sanitize-recover=all
    -fno-omit-frame-pointer
    -g
)

# URL parser fuzz target
add_executable(fuzz_url fuzz_parser.cpp)
target_compile_options(fuzz_url PRIVATE ${FUZZ_FLAGS})
target_link_options(fuzz_url PRIVATE ${FUZZ_FLAGS})

# Create corpus and dict directories
file(MAKE_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/corpus/url)
file(MAKE_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/corpus/json)
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# run_fuzz.sh — Run fuzzing with corpus and dictionary

set -e

TARGET=${1:-fuzz_url}
CORPUS_DIR="corpus/${TARGET}"
DICT_FILE="dictionaries/${TARGET}.dict"
TIMEOUT=${2:-60}  # seconds

# Create seed corpus with known-good inputs
mkdir -p "$CORPUS_DIR"
echo -n "http://example.com" > "$CORPUS_DIR/seed1"
echo -n "https://api.test.com:8080/path" > "$CORPUS_DIR/seed2"
echo -n "ftp://files.host.org/dir/file.txt" > "$CORPUS_DIR/seed3"

# Create dictionary with URL-relevant tokens
mkdir -p dictionaries
cat > "$DICT_FILE" << 'EOF'
"://"
"http"
"https"
"ftp"
"localhost"
":80"
":443"
":8080"
"/"
"?"
"&"
"="
"#"
"@"
EOF

# Run fuzzer
# -max_total_time: stop after N seconds
# -max_len: maximum input size
# -dict: token dictionary
# -jobs: parallel fuzzing
# -workers: number of worker processes
./$TARGET \
    "$CORPUS_DIR" \
    -max_total_time="$TIMEOUT" \
    -max_len=4096 \
    -dict="$DICT_FILE" \
    -jobs=4 \
    -workers=4 \
    -print_final_stats=1

echo "Corpus size: $(ls -1 $CORPUS_DIR | wc -l) inputs"
```
